intro: Monday opens with the first full week of GPT-5.5 in enterprise hands — adoption metrics are beating OpenAI's internal projections, the Pro tier is converting faster than standard among Fortune 500 buyers, and Anthropic has made its first post-$40B-investment product announcement. Tencent's DeepSeek stake has been confirmed at 18%, and Google's A2A protocol hit 500 registered partner integrations in its first 10 days.

top_stories.headline: GPT-5.5 Pro Outpaces Standard in Fortune 500 Adoption — Enterprise Buyers Self-Selecting Into Premium Tier
top_stories.summary: OpenAI's first-week GPT-5.5 metrics show the Pro tier ($30/M input) converting at a higher rate than standard ($5/M) among Fortune 500 enterprise buyers — the opposite of what most analysts predicted. Enterprise procurement teams are bypassing standard entirely, citing agentic computer use and complex workflow orchestration as the primary buying triggers that only Pro fully supports. Sam Altman said Pro adoption is 'well ahead of internal models' in a post on X, without disclosing specific numbers.

top_stories.headline: Tencent Confirms 18% DeepSeek Stake — WeChat AI Integration Announced for Q3 2026
top_stories.summary: Tencent formally confirmed an 18% equity stake in DeepSeek, making it the largest external shareholder. Simultaneously, Tencent announced DeepSeek V4 will be integrated natively into WeChat's 1.3 billion user ecosystem beginning Q3 2026, replacing the current Weixin bot with a full AI assistant layer. Alibaba Cloud separately confirmed a commercial agreement giving it preferred API access to DeepSeek V4 for enterprise cloud customers across Southeast Asia and the Middle East.

top_stories.headline: Anthropic Launches Claude Enterprise 3.0 — Compliance Layer for Healthcare, Finance, and Government
top_stories.summary: Anthropic's first major post-$40B product announcement is Claude Enterprise 3.0: a dedicated compliance and governance layer adding HIPAA BAA support, SOC 2 Type II certification, FedRAMP In-Process designation, and configurable data residency on top of Claude Opus 4.7. The product directly targets regulated industries where compliance is a non-negotiable procurement requirement and is priced at a significant premium over standard Claude Enterprise. Anthropic's Constitutional AI methodology is being productised into a compliance offering that competitors cannot replicate without 12-18 months of certification work.

top_stories.headline: Google A2A Protocol Reaches 500 Partner Integrations in 10 Days — Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, SAP Confirmed
top_stories.summary: Google's Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol has received confirmed integration commitments from over 500 partners in its first 10 days, including Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, SAP, and Zendesk. The $750M partner fund's first cohort of 47 recipients has been selected, each receiving between $5M and $25M for A2A-native agent development. Open-source A2A implementation libraries have appeared across GitHub in Python, TypeScript, and Go — community adoption accelerating beyond Google's official programme.

top_stories.headline: OpenAI Bio Safety Bug Bounty Draws 340+ Researchers in First Week — Critical Findings Under Review
top_stories.summary: OpenAI's $25,000 bio safety bug bounty programme (April 28–July 27) has attracted over 340 registered security researchers in its first week, with OpenAI confirming several preliminary findings are under active safety team review. The programme targets universal jailbreak detection in GPT-5.5's bio safety guardrails — the most adversarially tested safety layer in any commercial AI model. The volume of sign-ups signals the security research community views GPT-5.5's safety claims as a credible but testable target, which is the intended signal for regulated enterprise buyers.

quick_hits.text: Microsoft Copilot for Security processed its one-millionth enterprise security alert with AI triage in its first week of GA — a scale milestone Microsoft is using as the lead metric in its enterprise security sales motion.

Meta confirmed Llama 4 Maverick API availability in Meta AI Studio for enterprise fine-tuning and deployment — the clearest signal yet that Meta is building an enterprise AI API revenue stream alongside its consumer ad business.

India's AIGEG published its draft AI procurement framework for government agencies ahead of the May 31 deadline — requiring AI models used in government workflows to meet Mythos-equivalent benchmarks, effectively mandating a domestic evaluation process.
Perplexity crossed $500M ARR — up from $450M disclosed just 10 days ago — as Computer agent enterprise conversions accelerated during simultaneous evaluation of all three GA research agents.

Nvidia announced H200 NVL availability on Google Cloud, AWS, and Azure simultaneously — a multi-cloud synchronised launch responding to Google's TPU 8t advantage, giving enterprises on any hyperscaler access to Nvidia's current-generation inference hardware.

tools_products.name: Claude Enterprise 3.0
tools_products.what: Compliance and governance layer for regulated industries on Claude Opus 4.7 — HIPAA BAA, SOC 2 Type II, FedRAMP In-Process, configurable data residency; Anthropic's first post-$40B-investment product launch.

tools_products.name: DeepSeek V4 for WeChat
tools_products.what: Native DeepSeek V4 integration into WeChat's 1.3B user ecosystem launching Q3 2026 — the largest single AI assistant deployment in history by user count, following Tencent's confirmed 18% stake.

tools_products.name: Llama 4 Maverick Enterprise API
tools_products.what: Meta's frontier open-weight model now available via Meta AI Studio for enterprise fine-tuning and deployment on Meta infrastructure — Meta's most direct move yet into enterprise AI API revenue.

tools_products.name: A2A Protocol SDKs
tools_products.what: Community-built A2A implementation libraries in Python, TypeScript, and Go now on GitHub — adoption accelerating beyond Google's official 500-partner programme as developers implement independently.

tools_products.name: H200 NVL Multi-Cloud
tools_products.what: H200 NVL inference hardware now simultaneously available on Google Cloud, AWS, and Azure — Nvidia's direct response to Google TPU 8t, ensuring any hyperscaler customer has access to current-gen Nvidia inference.

india_roundup.text: India's AIGEG published its draft AI procurement framework — requiring government AI vendors to submit models for domestic evaluation against Mythos-equivalent benchmarks by July 2026, with final standards expected August 2026.

Sarvam AI, one of IndiaAI Mission's 12 shortlisted organisations, announced a NVIDIA partnership to train its next-generation multilingual foundation model covering 22 Indian languages — the largest sovereign language model training run in Indian history.

India's IT sector added 1.2 lakh net new jobs in April 2026, driven almost entirely by AI-augmented GCC expansion — the first month in three years where Indian IT net hiring grew faster than attrition.

PhonePe launched India's first AI-native UPI fraud detection system using a fine-tuned Krutrim model — processing 500M+ daily transactions with a 94% pre-completion fraud detection rate, a first for real-time payment AI in India.

India's DPDP Act implementation rules confirmed for Q2 2026 with a 90-day compliance window — AI companies are accelerating data localisation and consent management infrastructure purchases ahead of enforcement.

closing_insight: GPT-5.5 Pro converting Fortune 500 buyers faster than standard is the clearest signal yet that enterprise AI has stopped being a cost conversation and started being a capability conversation — and the buyers who understand their use case don't negotiate on price.
